articles.title: Musk Agrees to Pay $1.5 Million to Settle SEC Twitter Stake Case
articles.summary: Elon Musk agreed to settle Securities and Exchange Commission allegations that he cheated Twitter shareholders out of millions of dollars in 2022 by failing to properly disclose his growing stake in the social media company.

articles.title: 'It's an elite matter': UAE confirms it's in talks for swap line loan with US
articles.summary: 'It's an elite matter': UAE confirms it's in talks for swap line loan with US The  UAE  confirmed on Monday that it is discussing a currency swap line with the US, framing it as part of entry into an “elite group” of US allies, as fresh  Iranian air strikes  roiled the country. "We have this discussion and conversation with many, it's part of an elite group that the US is having this swap policy with. They are only having it with five countries," UAE Trade Minister Thani bin Ahmed al-Zeyoudi said at a conference in Abu Dhabi. "Being part of that group means that transaction

articles.title: Pretium CEO: Capital for Home Construction is Drying up
articles.summary: Pretium CEO Don Mullen discusses the impacts of the 21st Century Road to Housing Act, home construction capital drying up, and the need to speed up homebuilding. He talks with Katie Greifeld and Romaine Bostick at the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills, California. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: SEC Investigating Alleged Fraud in Private Credit, Atkins Says
articles.summary: Securities and Exchange Commission Chairman Paul Atkins said the agency is investigating allegations of fraud in private credit firms, though he declined to say specifically which companies are under scrutiny.

articles.title: Iran denies plan to target UAE, blames US for 'adventurism'
articles.summary: Iran denies plan to target UAE, blames US for 'adventurism' Iranian state media says there was no planned attack on oil facilities at Fujairah port in the United Arab Emirates, citing a military official. The official said the incident was not premeditated and attributed it to US actions in the Strait of Hormuz. They described it as "the result of U.S. military's adventurism to create passage for illegal ship transit" through the waterway.

articles.title: Israeli strikes kill at least two in Gaza, Wafa reports
articles.summary: Israeli strikes kill at least two in Gaza, Wafa reports At least two people have been killed in Israeli attacks across Gaza on Monday, according to the Wafa. One strike targeted the central Bureij refugee camp, where a drone attack killed one person and wounded several others. A separate air strike hit an area southeast of Gaza City, killing another person and injuring multiple civilians. The latest attacks come despite the October 10 ceasefire, with at least 834 people killed in Gaza since the truce took effect.

The emir criticised the strikes, saying they targeted civilian sites and infrastructure. “He expressed ‘strong condemnation of the Iranian attacks targeting civilian sites and facilities in the sisterly United Arab Emirates using missiles and drones’,” the statement said. articles.summary: UAE says it intercepted 15 missiles and four drones launched from Iran The UAE Ministry of Defence says its air defences shot down multiple projectiles launched from Iran on Monday. articles.title: Lebanon death toll rises to 2,696 as Israeli attacks continue
articles.summary: Lebanon death toll rises to 2,696 as Israeli attacks continue The number of people killed in Israel’s war on Lebanon since 2 March has climbed to 2,696, with 8,264 others wounded, Lebanon's official National News Agency reported. Israeli air strikes killed several civilians on Monday as warplanes carried out intensive raids across towns in southern Lebanon.

articles.title: Trump pleads with South Korea to join US mission to protect Gulf shipping
articles.summary: Trump pleads with South Korea to join US mission to protect Gulf shipping US President Donald Trump has called on South Korea to help secure shipping routes near Iran. Trump said Washington is seeking broader international participation to bail the US from what has become another war of choice in the Middle East. He added that US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth will hold a news conference on Tuesday alongside General Dan Caine to provide further details on the operation.

articles.summary: Oil surges past $115 after reported drone strike on UAE port Oil prices continue their upward incline, with Brent crude climbing to fresh intraday highs after reports of a drone strike targeting a key oil facility in the United Arab Emirates. Brent rose above $115 a barrel shortly after 17:00 BST, gaining more than five percent on the day, following reports that the port of Fujairah had come under attack. He added that Iran is not seeking conflict but will respond forcefully if it is drawn into one. articles.title: US says it destroyed Iranian boats, Iran denies naval losses
articles.summary: US says it destroyed Iranian boats, Iran denies naval losses The US says its forces have destroyed six Iranian small boats and intercepted cruise missiles and drones during a new operation aimed at securing shipping routes through the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian officials rejected the US account. State television quoted a military official denying that any Iranian boats had been sunk, directly contradicting the US claims. US Admiral Brad Cooper, the head of Central Command, said on Monday that US forces carried out the action as part of efforts to keep the waterway open amid escalating tensions.
